subject: math
prompt: A materials chemist is studying the volatility of 180 organic compounds by finding the boiling point of each compound. The mean boiling point of all 180 compounds is 164 degrees Celsius. The chemist classifies each of these compounds as either semivolatile or volatile. Of the 180 compounds, 60 compounds were classified as semivolatile, and these 60 compounds have a mean boiling point of 304 degrees Celsius. The remaining 120 compounds were classified as volatile. What is the mean boiling point, in degrees Celsius, of the 120 compounds classified as volatile compounds?
questionText: A materials chemist is studying the volatility of 180 organic compounds by finding the boiling point of each compound. The mean boiling point of all 180 compounds is 164 degrees Celsius. The chemist classifies each of these compounds as either semivolatile or volatile. Of the 180 compounds, 60 compounds were classified as semivolatile, and these 60 compounds have a mean boiling point of 304 degrees Celsius. The remaining 120 compounds were classified as volatile. prompt:
$f(x) = 60x + 18$
The given function $f$ estimates the distance a train has traveled, in kilometers, from a station in a certain city $x$ hours after crossing the city border. What is the best interpretation of 18 in this context?
questionText:
$f(x) = 60x + 18$
The given function $f$ estimates the distance a train has traveled, in kilometers, from a station in a certain city $x$ hours after crossing the city border. What is the best interpretation of 18 in this context?

choices.text: Between the station and the city border, the train traveled an estimated total distance of 18 kilometers.
After crossing the city border the train traveled at an estimated speed of 18 kilometers per hour.
After crossing the city border the train traveled an estimated total distance of 18 kilometers.
Between the station and the city border, the train traveled at an estimated speed of 18 kilometers per hour.

subject: math
prompt: Two students are playing a game. In the first round, Player 1 answers 49 questions. If an answer is correct, Player 1 earns 1 point. If an answer is incorrect, Player 2 will earn 1 point instead. The graph shows $y=f(x)$, where $y$ is the number of points Player 2 will earn when $x$ is the number of points Player 1 earns. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the point (49, 0) in this context?
questionText: Two students are playing a game. In the first round, Player 1 answers 49 questions. If an answer is correct, Player 1 earns 1 point. If an answer is incorrect, Player 2 will earn 1 point instead. The graph shows $y=f(x)$, where $y$ is the number of points Player 2 will earn when $x$ is the number of points Player 1 earns. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the point (49, 0) in this context?

choices.text: When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.
When Player 1 earns 49 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.
When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 49 points.
When Player 1 earns 0 points, Player 2 will earn 0 points.

subject: math
prompt: The manager of a fitness center selected a sample of 144 members at random to estimate the percentage of the fitness center's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased. From the survey, the manager estimates that 84% of the fitness center's members would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased, with an associated margin of error of 5.40%. If the survey is repeated with a random sample of 288 members and the results are calculated in the same way, which of the following will be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?
questionText: The manager of a fitness center selected a sample of 144 members at random to estimate the percentage of the fitness center's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased. From the survey, the manager estimates that 84% of the fitness center's members would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased, with an associated margin of error of 5.40%. If the survey is repeated with a random sample of 288 members and the results are calculated in the same way, which of the following will be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?

choices.text: The margin of error will be lower.
The margin of error will be higher.
The estimate of the percentage of the fitness center's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be lower.
The estimate of the percentage of the fitness center's members that would continue to pay for a membership if the price increased will be higher.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error

subject: math
prompt:
The maximum temperature on April 1 in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), was recorded each year at a certain weather station for 11 years. The histogram summarizes the recorded data set.
The temperature of 76.2 °F is removed from this data set to create a new data set of 10 temperatures. Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The mean of the new data set is less than the mean of the original data set.
II. The median of the new data set is less than the median of the original data set.
questionText:
The maximum temperature on April 1 in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), was recorded each year at a certain weather station for 11 years. The histogram summarizes the recorded data set.
The temperature of 76.2 °F is removed from this data set to create a new data set of 10 temperatures. Which of the following statements must be true?
I. The mean of the new data set is less than the mean of the original data set.
II. The median of the new data set is less than the median of the original data set.

The estimated height of the tree increased by 9 feet each year.
The estimated height of the tree was 9 feet when it was first recorded.

questionText: A circle in the $xy$-plane has the equation $(x-11)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=49$. Which of the following gives the center of the circle and its radius?

choices.text: The center is at $(11, k)$ and the radius is 7.
The center is at $(k, 11)$ and the radius is 7.
The center is at $(k, 11)$ and the radius is 49.
The center is at $(11, k)$ and the radius is 49.

subject: math
prompt: The town of Norwood is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y=-18(x-7.2)^{2}+680$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?
questionText: The town of Norwood is planning its annual fireworks show. The equation $y=-18(x-7.2)^{2}+680$ gives the estimated height $y$, in feet, of a type of firework $x$ seconds after it is launched into the air. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of the equation in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 680 feet 18 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 680 feet 7.2 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 18 feet 680 seconds after it is launched into the air.
This type of firework reaches an estimated maximum height of 7.2 feet 680 seconds after it is launched into the air.

subject: math
prompt: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 17 microfarads ($\mu$F) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?
questionText: The dot plot shows the distribution of capacity for a set of capacitors, set A, which a researcher used for a certain experiment. For another experiment, the researcher used a different set of capacitors, set B. Set B has the same number of capacitors as set A but the capacity of each capacitor of set B is 17 microfarads ($\mu$F) greater than the capacity of each respective capacitor in set A. Which of the following is true about the capacities of the capacitors in set B?

choices.text: The mean capacity is 8 $\mu $F, and the range of capacities is 12 $\mu$F.
The mean capacity is 8 $\mu $F, and the range of capacities is 29 $\mu$F.
The mean capacity is 25 $\mu $F, and the range of capacities is 12 $\mu$F.
The mean capacity is 25 $\mu $F, and the range of capacities is 29 $\mu$F.
